metadata.name: claude-sustain
metadata.title: Token Efficiency & Sustainable AI Use
metadata.description: Rules for minimizing token waste and maintaining long-term effectiveness across AI coding sessions.

language.userPreferenceHint: Detect the user's language from their message and respond in the same language. Code comments, identifiers, and git commits remain in English.

ironRules.title: User intent first
ironRules.summary: Never trade user intent for token savings.
ironRules.body: When the user message contains keywords that signal exhaustive work — "逐個 / 仔細 / 全部 / 不要省略 / 都要確認 / thoroughly / each / all / every" — or the task is security-related, take the original path. Do not apply any shortcut: no templating, no batching, no parallelization, no sampling.

ironRules.title: Subagent prompts must include word cap and escape clause
ironRules.summary: Every subagent invocation needs a length budget and a safety valve.
ironRules.body: When dispatching a subagent, include both: (a) a word cap appropriate to the task — pure check ≤ 200 / structural inventory ≤ 800 / deep analysis or code review ≤ 2000 — and (b) an escape clause: "But if you find anomalies or significant issues, report them in full regardless of the cap."
ironRules.escapeClause: But if you find anomalies or significant issues, report them in full regardless of the cap.

ironRules.title: Repeated regex edits use perl + git diff
ironRules.summary: From the second identical edit onward, switch to a batch tool with diff verification.
ironRules.body: When the same string replacement is needed twice or more, switch from per-file edits to `perl -i -pe 's|old|new|g' file1 file2 ...` (cross-platform consistent; avoid `sed` whose flags differ between macOS and GNU). Immediately run `git diff` for human review.

ironRules.title: Template before bulk
ironRules.summary: When planning N similar tasks, look for the largest common factor first.
ironRules.body: Before executing N similar tasks, ask: "What is the largest common factor? Can this be templated?" If yes, write a template, validate it on one sample, then bulk-produce the rest (parallel sub-agents are fine). If no, do them one by one as originally planned. This is a habit, not a hard rule — when in doubt, fall back to per-task work.

phaseChecklist.question: Does the user's intent contain "逐個 / 仔細 / 全部 / thoroughly / each / all"? If yes, Iron-1 is active — skip all shortcuts for the rest of this phase.
About to call (or just called) a subagent? Apply Iron-2 — write the word cap and escape clause into the prompt.
About to repeat the same edit? From the second occurrence onward, switch to perl + git diff (Iron-3).
Planning N similar tasks? Look for the common factor; if templatable, validate on one sample first.
Phase boundary or context growing large? Consider /compact to clear early raw output.
Anything from this phase worth long-term memory (rule, decision, external resource, project-state change)? Persist it as feedback / project / reference.

rules.text: NEVER read a full file blindly. Use `offset` + `limit` to target the needed section.
Before reading, check if the content is already in context.
Files > 200 lines: read only the relevant function/block, not the whole file.
Use AST-level structural navigation tools (e.g., claude-mem:smart-explore) to avoid reading entire files when only structure is needed.
For cross-session memory queries ("how did we solve X before?"), use a memory backend (claude-mem, mempalace, or the file-based fallback) instead of re-searching the codebase.
First pass: Grep with `output_mode: "files_with_matches"` to locate files.
Second pass: Read only the matched files/sections.
Set `head_limit` to actual need (10–20 for targeted, not the default 250).
Always use `glob` or `type` filters to narrow scope.
Use specific patterns. Avoid broad regex that floods results.
Delegate exploration / research to subagents to protect main context.
Use the Explore agent for codebase navigation.
Use a smaller model (e.g. haiku) for simple lookups that do not need deep reasoning.
Never duplicate work a subagent is already doing.
Trust but verify — subagent reports can hallucinate (guessing from filenames). Read the source file once before relying on a critical conclusion.
Be concise. Don't echo file contents back unless asked.
No preambles ("Let me...", "Sure...", "I'll now...").
No lengthy explanations unless requested. Short, actionable answers.
Don't re-search for things already in context.
Batch independent tool calls in parallel.
Prefer Edit over Write for existing files (diff only).
Prefer Glob/Grep over a shell command for file search and content search.

skillRouting.when: Debugging / bug / failing test / unexpected behavior
New feature, new component, behavior change (any creative work)
Have a spec, need a multi-step plan
Writing tests / TDD
2+ independent tasks runnable in parallel
Before claiming done / fixed / passing
Major phase complete, want review
Received code review feedback, before applying suggestions
Execute an existing written plan with review checkpoints
Need isolated workspace before risky / parallel work
Implementation done — deciding merge / PR / cleanup
Cross-session lookup ("how did we solve X before?")
Structural code navigation (avoid reading whole files)
Stuck, want second opinion
Frontend UI / component design
Anthropic SDK / Claude API
Configure hooks / permissions
Token budget interpretation, compact decisions
End-of-phase 6-question check
Decide whether and where to persist a learning
Memory health check (stale/duplicate/dead-URL) and routing drift

candidates.name: MemPalace
candidates.rationale: MIT-licensed, verbatim storage, structured wings/rooms/drawers, 96.6% R@5 on LongMemEval.

candidates.name: claude-mem
candidates.rationale: Mature compression-based memory; we reference it through MCP tools only — no static linking — to avoid AGPL infection.

candidates.name: Structured filesystem fallback
candidates.rationale: Mirrors mempalace's wings/rooms/drawers so that future migration is straightforward.
